_comment: Per-user write-contract cache for the Notion bases. Copy to schema.cache.json (git-ignored) or let `bootstrap-notion` write it on setup. `sort-inbox` reads this instead of discovering field names / formats / select values through failed writes. Per base: `title` = the title-property name to write the row name into; `dateFields` = date properties (write them with the `date:<Field>:start` form); `selects` = property -> allowed option values (writing anything else errors). Top-level `areas` maps Area name -> the FULL Notion URL of the Area row (relations need the full URL, not a bare id). `reviews.currentRow` caches the active period row so the weekly Reviews lookup is skipped while the period label still matches. Replace every placeholder with real values for your workspace; `bootstrap-notion` fills these automatically.

_format_notes.dates: Write date fields as "date:<Field>:start": "YYYY-MM-DD" (e.g. "date:Do date:start"). A bare "Do date" errors with 'Date type must be expanded'.
_format_notes.relations: Area (and any relation) must be the FULL row URL from `areas` below, never a bare page id ('Invalid agent URL').
_format_notes.selects: Only values listed under `selects` are valid. Free-text like 'December 2026' into Goals.Horizon errors with 'Invalid select value'.
_format_notes.title: Use the exact `title` field name per base ('Property Name not found' means you used the wrong title field, e.g. 'Name' instead of 'Task').

Reviews._comment: Wide format: one row = one period, with a text column per area. No append command — fetch the row, read the column, update with the full new value.
Reviews.title: Period

areas._comment: Area name (without emoji prefix) -> FULL URL of the Area row in Areas DB. Canonical: Career, Health, Sport, Money, Family, Content, Other. Areas DB is canonical and effectively never changes, so this map can be cached indefinitely.

reviews._comment: Cache of the active Reviews period row. If `periodLabel` still matches the current period, reuse `id` and skip the lookup; otherwise find/create the row and refresh both fields.
